OPENAI IS RESTARTING ITS ROBOTICS RESEARCH GROUP
articles.summary: OpenAI is reviving its robotics research group after a three-year hiatus, aiming to develop multimodal robotics models and improve core AI models.

articles.title: AI OVERVIEWS: ABOUT LAST WEEK
articles.summary: Google introduced AI Overviews to enhance search results, providing users with more accurate and relevant information, especially for complex queries. While some issues arose, such as erroneous results and misinterpreted content, Google has implemented over a dozen technical improvements to address these challenges, including better detection of nonsensical queries and limiting user-generated content in AI Overviews.

articles.title: NVIDIA SAID TO BE PREPPING AI PC CHIP WITH ARM AND BLACKWELL CORES
articles.summary: Nvidia is reportedly preparing a system-on-chip that pairs Arm's Cortex-X5 core design with GPUs based on Nvidia's Blackwell architecture.

articles.title: MUP FOR SPARSE MODELS
articles.summary: muP is a powerful tool used by all frontier model labs to transfer hyper parameters tuned on small models to larger, more expensive training runs. This paper explores how to do that for sparse models, thus dramatically improving training performance while reducing compute costs.

articles.title: EXPERIMENTING WITH LOCAL ALT TEXT GENERATION
articles.summary: Mozilla has trained a model that can run in the browser and automatically generate alt text for web images.

articles.title: RANDOM COLOR ERASING BOOSTS MODEL ROBUSTNESS IN COMPUTER VISION
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a new learning strategy called Random Color Erasing to tackle color bias in computer vision. This method balances the importance of color and other features by selectively removing color information from training data, leading to models that perform better in complex scenarios like wide-area surveillance and industrial applications.

articles.title: account
VIDEO GENERATION WITH MORA
articles.summary: Mora is an innovative video generation model that leverages multiple visual AI agents to challenge Sora, the leading model by OpenAI.

articles.title: BOOSTING LLM'S ABILITY TO FOLLOW COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS
articles.summary: Conifer improves LLMs' understanding of complex instructions by introducing a specialized dataset and a progressive learning approach.

articles.title: FABRIC FEEDBACK TO IMPROVE IMAGE GENERATION QUALITY
articles.summary: FABRIC is an inference time, training-free method for improving image quality by using a scoring function or image examples to direct output quality.

articles.title: KL IS ALL YOU NEED
articles.summary: KL divergence is a fast, cheap, and powerful way to measure a kind of distance between things. It is used copiously in traditional and modern AI. This post explores the powerful concept visually and mathematically.

articles.title: BEYOND THE CLOUD TO EDGE AI
articles.summary: AI is moving to multimodal and the edge. How will that change our use of it and where it is effective?

articles.title: EX-OPENAI BOARD MEMBER REVEALS WHAT LED TO SAM ALTMAN'S BRIEF OUSTING
articles.summary: Former OpenAI board member Helen Toner revealed new details about what led to CEO Sam Altman's ousting in November in a recent interview. The board had apparently learned about ChatGPT's release on Twitter. Toner says that Altman had lied to the board multiple times. Altman was reportedly withholding information and misrepresenting things that were happening in the company for years. His lies made it difficult for the board to make decisions, leading them to think that he wasn't the right person to lead the company to AGI.

articles.title: LLM MERGING CHALLENGE
articles.summary: Sakana AI is sponsoring the LLM Merging challenge at NeurIPS this year.

articles.title: TRIBECA TO SCREEN SORA-GENERATED FILMS
articles.summary: The 2024 Tribeca Festival will feature "Sora Shorts," showcasing five AI-generated short films created using OpenAI's text-to-video model Sora.

articles.title: 7 RETENTION STRATEGIES FOR AI COMPANIES
articles.summary: A guide for founders and product leaders that includes examples of how companies like Perplexity, Civit, Lapse, Omnivore, and others are leveraging them to boost retention.
